texto: If you had come to me a hundred years ago, do you think I should have dreamed of the telephone? Why, even now I cannot understand it! I use it every day, I transact half my correspondence by means of it, but I don’t understand it. Think of that little stretched disk of iron at the end of a wire repeating in your ear not only sounds, but words—not only words, but all the most delicate and elusive inflections and nuances of tone which separate one human voice from another!
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de William Crookes que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “If you had come to me a hundred years ago, do you think I should have dreamed of the telephone? W…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre motivacao, dream, imagination, com foco em termino e imagination.
